item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whelp, I finally got to the one that started SCP R34, and it did not disappoint.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it is possible due to William waving at Capone, eventually resulting in Capone waving back.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was idly wondering if learning and then teaching Mal0 sign language would allow you to communicate with them…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
># Really good job and awesome picture.
># I really liked it

End post.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's my favourite SCP.It's the first SCP that scared me

item.title: caution
item.content:encoded: Ah yes the article that started many rule34 art of.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it bad if I wanted to download Malo if the SCP Foundation was real….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love it. I hope one day we have a MalO app that randomly places a MalO in a random picture of a place. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: who was the first person to look at this vaguely humanoid nightmare wolf with a skull for a face and think "i wanna bang that"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There is so much porn of it because it's some ones fursona. lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I expected a little more then this. But then, writing aynthing more would ruin it.

Dunno why there is so much porn of exactly this SCP.

I guess that's rule 34 for you.

item.title: Re: Im sorry
item.content:encoded: What do you mean by this exactly? As far as I'm aware this has never happened.

item.title: Im sorry
item.content:encoded: I feel bad for the writer it's not your fault there are horrible people in this world who want to errmmm harass animals I'm super super super sorry for their behavior I swear it is not the majority of people in this community

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All of the what

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I made a tale kinda like that just now, actually.

item.title: Anyone else actually want it?
item.content:encoded: Legit, I'd like a friend, and possibly someone to play pranks with. Imagine manifesting him somewhere you can't go to fiddle with something, like inside a school to change the PA system to speak in a darth vader voice, or to help you out if you're getting mugged. If nothing else, I'll have someone to talk to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
dr bright is a menace to society
+1 for the scp tho

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So you're telling me… all I gotta do to write an scp… is write a creepy skull ass looking thing and then people will want to fornicate with it? Sign me up

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Doesn't Dr. Bright want to make a cat girl based off this? If so then I support it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Still in awe that the creator of the costume is Clockwork Creations. The character's official name, Breynz, is still one of my favorite works by CC. Only recently discovered that this is the same suit as Breynz.

No uncollapsed images on the forums, please. -Zyn
hide

Yee Haw

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember the days when this SCP terrified me. When I read it and glanced around the room to make sure that thing wasn't in my room. When I dreaded looking through my phone's gallery.

Before all the god-awful porn of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oh

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1471 is to be terminated as soon as possible.
Reason: pulling a fuckin sneaky on us

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cool concept and nice execution. But there’s another thing I’d like to say: PSA to all furries: do not lewd a horrifying wolf thing. You chose among one of the worst things to make hentai of. At least lewd something else for Christ’s sake. Jesus.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Enjoy the reddit karma? Got my upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

*sigh*

scp-1471 got us all and we didnt even notice….

item.title: Licensing Team Post
item.content:encoded: After long consideration, I have decided that certain posts by the rightsholder are equivalent to, admittedly post-hoc, consent. The image has been restored.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really sad about the picture removal. It was the thing bringing the whole article together.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There isn't really any established procedure for what to do with civilians who manage to get to >90. Amnestics? It sorta seems like trusting they'll be treated like lunatics is a bad idea, because they know the app's name and effects.

The only issue is the tale. It clearly shows how exposed civilians with knowledge of the SCP and its effects will contribute to the spreading of the app. But changing procedures with civilians will make that tale improbable now. It could be one of the earliest cases, I guess, and the Foundation could assume they've got civilian exposure at negligible levels so maybe you don't need to change it, but it seems like carelessness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: anybody else look for this entry after finding it's one of the most rule34 done creatures

item.title: Re: Image removed
item.content:encoded:
Thought this was resolved some 2 years prior, but here's the discussion on it.

http://05command.wikidot.com/forum/t-1136954

UPDATE:

Issues resolved.

item.title: Image removed
item.content:encoded:
So looks like the SCP's image didn't fit into some copyright rulings and got removed.
Not much else I can say. I wish there was a place to discuss this, and maybe try to find a replacement image for this article.

item.title: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded: Given that the image is copyrighted, we're going to work on getting permission from the person in that costume. In the mean time this copyrighted image has been taken down.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Or maybe the instances are kids of something, and destroying a lot of the phones will trigger its wrath…

item.title: Re: Canid-like skull
item.content:encoded: Or maybe it doesn't match any known breeds or species, but still shares enough anatomical features to be considered a canine?

item.title: Re: Is Capone canon?
item.content:encoded:
Hahahaha… That dissonance came to my attention immediatly after reading Capone.

The thing just stares at you and waves back, yet he is named Mal0

item.title: Re: Is Capone canon?
item.content:encoded: Because Buen0 was already taken

item.title: Is Capone canon?
item.content:encoded:
The face is disturbing, and has been making me dream with it for 2 days and wishing to get a mask…

I would like to know if you guys regard Capone as canon, as it brings some interesting implications. If it is, why name it Mal0?

item.title: Re: The app's purpose
item.content:encoded: awww

item.title: The app's purpose
item.content:encoded:
I just noticed that the app's description can almost make it sound like it's purpose was not to help users with their social anxiety and loneliness but rather to help the monster with it's social anxiety and loneliness.
That is to say it seems like the app was made to help scp-1471-a come out of it's shell and act as an icebreaker for meeting new people, the reason attempts at communication have failed is because it's shy.

item.title: This has been rule 34'd so much by furries
item.content:encoded: +1, of course, this is a great classic, but don't google image this…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Soneone needs to make a real verion of MalO 1.0.0. I would, personally, enjoy the images. I'm weird. =3

I enjoyed this article very much, nonetheless! It's short but sweet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's also a good in-universe reason for that policy- If containment is working, don't fix break what isn't broken. (I always like to point at SCP-065 and SCP-1609, in which the GOC and Foundation play Goofus and Gallant)

Right now, -A is non-hostile sentient entity, and possibly even a friendly sapient one. It just happens to be creepy looking and have a poor grasp on the concept of "privacy". For all the Foundation knows, destroying the phones might injure or cause pain to SCP-1471-A. Heck, if there's a unique -A for each phone, destroying the phone could effectively kill that -A instance. Even the friendliest entity is probably going to be upset by either one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, it's not in their policy to destroy scips if they can avoid it. That's why it's 'Secure, Contain, Protect', not 'Secure, Think About It, Destroy'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Any reason the foundation doesn't just keep say twenty of these devices in storage and destroy the rest? i see no reason for an ever growing pile of SCP's, you know what this does and how to contain it, but in case of perimeter/containment breaches you'd rather not them being able to be distributed en masse. Twenty missing devices are relocatable, especially when fitted with GPS and RFID, five hundred or more are not.

item.title: Re: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded: Since edits are made and I notice the 'crosslink' tab is still on, I will remove it since it has no need for it (as far as I can tell).

item.title: Re: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded:
Ehh

Fan of linking to the tale, not a fan of the placement, but there's also no where else that seems good to link it.

If LurkD allows it I would think an extra line or two before the app description paragraph to link to the tale would be perfect

item.title: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Stopped. Edits made.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ show block
– hide block

I propose the following edit:

Description:
For ████████████. Never settle for those awkward feelings of being alone ever again. MalO is an exciting and interactive experience that will keep you engaged and intrigued. The anxiety of social situations can be nerve-racking, but after just a few hours of MalO you will soon forget all about those painful emotions of disappointment. Be part of the new craze that is quickly becoming the next social substitute. Remember, the more you participate, the more MalO will engage you. Your experience is completely up to you. Absolutely NO ADS. Enjoy!

Buy?

To this:

Description:
For ████████████. Never settle for those awkward feelings of being alone ever again. MalO is an exciting and interactive experience that will keep you engaged and intrigued. The anxiety of social situations can be nerve-racking, but after just a few hours of MalO you will soon forget all about those painful emotions of disappointment. Be part of the new craze that is quickly becoming the next social substitute. Remember, the more you participate, the more MalO will engage you. Your experience is completely up to you. Absolutely NO ADS. Enjoy!

Buy?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Search Merle on this site.

+ Spoilers
-  You were warned

LurkD's character actually is in the final stage (>90 hours) of exposure and taught him sign language. Turn out that Merle/1471 is a d**k/goofball, which just makes him even cuter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well… that got out of hand rather quickly… that genuinely sent a chill down my spine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my goodness. Okay, this SCP has massive appeal to me both for being pretty unsettling and for the creature generated (I'm gonna call it Malo) being very appealing to my aesthetic tastes, haha. I have a whole tag on my Tumblr for animal skeletons and similar things, so I dunno how much Malo here would actually bother me. In practice the article is still very unsettling though, even to someone like me whose tastes accommodate monster designs like this very well.

One thing that would be kinda cool though- it'd be interesting if once the consistent manifestations began, Malo would become more physical after a while, so someone like, say, Soulless back on page 2 (or me, ngl) actually could have physical contact with it.

…Now that I look at the article again, it wasn't actually stated whether or not it eventually becomes physical once it begins to manifest persistently, huh. I'm gonna roll with it eventually being possible, headcanons and all that, but I imagine cases are snubbed well before it gets to that point.

Anyway, +1.

item.title: Re: Canid-like skull
item.content:encoded: Perhaps because they don't have a specimen to work with. As 1471-A is effectively just an image, it is guesswork at best to determine its features. Maybe they have a biologist's opinion, but they don't have a specimen to verify it properly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This seems like something the foundation would experiment with. It's never been shown to be hostile, so why not use d-class to figure out what it is communicating?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really want to know what is it trying to communicate. Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is good, and creepy, and it uses the image well for not-super-cliche ends (and the ad is great!). That said, it would be hilarious if it actually DID have ads. Extremely personalized ads. And you can never get rid of it.

Ever.

Granted, that would send it straight from "creepy" to "annoying as hell," but it would be hilarious as well!

Upvoted anyways.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is creepy! I would not want to buy that app!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this SCP, buuuuuut…

Doesn't it seem kinda like 513?

item.title: Canid-like skull
item.content:encoded:
Wouldn't the Foundation have a biologist on its ranks, who would be entirely capable of determining what kind of skull it is?
It feels very weird to me when it is described like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so perfect XD. giant weird dog thing that doesn't hurt anyone. I really just want to get a costume like that and make a video of people looking up from texts to see it waving/staring "dreamily"/dancing in front of them and then getting sad when they run away.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because there are instances of it outside of containment and as such is not reliably contained.
